items.title: Hide Apache Version and OS Information
items.description: Prevent Apache from revealing version number, OS details, and module information in error pages and headers.
items.threat: Information Disclosure - Version numbers help attackers find known exploits.
items.verification: Server header does not show version

implementation.apache:
# In apache2.conf or httpd.conf:
ServerTokens Prod
ServerSignature Off

testing: Server header shows only 'Apache' without version
Error pages don't show version/OS info
items.rollback: Remove or comment out ServerTokens and ServerSignature directives.

items.title: Disable HTTP TRACE Method
items.description: Disable TRACE HTTP method to prevent Cross-Site Tracing (XST) attacks.
items.threat: XST Attack - TRACE can be used to steal cookies even with HttpOnly flag.
items.verification: TRACE requests return 405 Method Not Allowed

implementation.apache:
# In apache2.conf or virtual host:
TraceEnable off

testing: curl -X TRACE returns 405 Method Not Allowed
No TRACE method in Allow header
items.rollback: Set TraceEnable On or remove directive.

items.title: Disable ETag Headers
items.description: Remove ETag headers to prevent information leakage about inode numbers.
items.threat: Information Disclosure - ETags can reveal inode numbers useful for attacks.
items.verification: No ETag header in HTTP responses
items.rollback: Remove FileETag directive or set to default.

items.title: Add Security Headers
items.threat: XSS, Clickjacking, Protocol Downgrade - Missing headers enable attacks.
items.impact: Cross-site scripting, frame injection, downgrade attacks.
items.verification: Security headers present in all responses

implementation.apache:
# In apache2.conf or .htaccess:
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block"
Header always set X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff"
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains"

mitigationSteps: 3. Configure HSTS only for HTTPS sites

items.title: Implement Content Security Policy
items.description: Define CSP header to prevent XSS and data injection attacks.
items.threat: XSS, Data Injection - Without CSP, malicious scripts can execute.
items.impact: Cross-site scripting, credential theft, session hijacking.
items.verification: CSP header present and blocking unauthorized resources

implementation.apache:
# Start with report-only:
Header always set Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self'; font-src 'self'"

# Then enforce:
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; form-action 'self'; base-uri 'self';"

mitigationSteps: 3. Adjust policy to allow legitimate resources
items.rollback: Remove CSP header or return to report-only mode.

items.title: Disable Directory Listing
items.description: Prevent Apache from showing directory contents when index file is missing.
items.threat: Information Disclosure - Directory listings reveal file structure and potentially sensitive files.
items.impact: Attackers discover files, backups, configuration files.
mitigationSteps: 3. Alternatively ensure index files exist in all directories
5. Test by visiting directory without index
items.rollback: Change to Options +Indexes or remove directive.

items.title: Disable Symbolic Links Following
items.description: Prevent Apache from following symbolic links which could lead to directory traversal.
items.threat: Directory Traversal - Symlinks can expose files outside web root.
items.impact: Unauthorized access to sensitive system files.

implementation.apache:
# In apache2.conf:
Options -FollowSymLinks
# Or use more secure:
Options -SymLinksIfOwnerMatch

items.title: Limit .htaccess Overrides
items.description: Restrict which directives can be overridden in .htaccess files.
items.threat: Configuration Tampering - Malicious .htaccess can enable dangerous features.
items.impact: Server compromise, PHP execution in upload directories.

implementation.apache:
# In Directory block:
AllowOverride None

# Or limit to specific:
AllowOverride AuthConfig Limit

# For uploads directory:
<Directory /var/www/html/uploads>
    AllowOverride None
    php_flag engine off
</Directory>

mitigationSteps: 2. Set AllowOverride None in sensitive directories
testing: Auth directives still work if needed
items.rollback: Set AllowOverride All to restore original behavior.

items.title: Configure Connection Timeouts
items.description: Set reasonable timeouts to prevent slowloris attacks and resource exhaustion.
items.threat: DoS Attacks - Slow connections can exhaust server resources.

implementation.apache:
# In apache2.conf:
Timeout 60
KeepAliveTimeout 5
RequestReadTimeout header=20-40,MinRate=500 body=20,MinRate=500

items.rollback: Revert timeout values to previous settings.

items.title: Enable and Configure ModSecurity
items.description: Deploy ModSecurity WAF with OWASP Core Rule Set for protection against web attacks.
items.threat: Web Attacks - Without WAF, vulnerable to SQLi, XSS, RCE.
items.impact: Data breach, site compromise, malware infection.

implementation.apache:
# Install:
apt install libapache2-mod-security2 modsecurity-crs

# Enable:
a2enmod security2

# Configure in /etc/modsecurity/modsecurity.conf:
SecRuleEngine On
SecRequestBodyLimit 13107200
SecResponseBodyLimit 524288

items.rollback: Set SecRuleEngine DetectionOnly or disable module.

items.title: Configure SSL/TLS Securely
items.description: Use strong SSL protocols and ciphers, disable weak algorithms.
items.threat: Man-in-the-Middle - Weak SSL allows interception and decryption.
items.impact: Session hijacking, credential theft, data exposure.

implementation.apache:
# In SSL config:
SSLEngine on
SSLProtocol all -SSLv3 -TLSv1 -TLSv1.1
SSLCipherSuite ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384
SSLHonorCipherOrder on
SSLCompression off
SSLSessionTickets off
Header always set Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload"

items.rollback: Revert SSL configuration to previous state.

items.title: Configure Comprehensive Logging
items.description: Enable detailed access and error logging with proper rotation.
items.threat: Insufficient Logging - Without logs, attacks go undetected.
items.impact: Delayed breach detection, no forensic evidence.
items.verification: All requests logged with relevant details

implementation.apache:
# In apache2.conf:
LogLevel warn
LogFormat "%h %l %u %t \"%r\" %>s %b \"%{Referer}i\" \"%{User-Agent}i\" %D %{SSL_PROTOCOL}x %{SSL_CIPHER}x" combined
CustomLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/access.log combined
ErrorLog ${APACHE_LOG_DIR}/error.log

# For mod_security:
SecAuditEngine RelevantOnly
SecAuditLog /var/log/apache2/modsec_audit.log

mitigationSteps: 1. Configure LogFormat with all needed fields
3. Set LogLevel to warn or info
7. Forward logs to SIEM if available
testing: Access logs contain all requested fields
Error logs capture warnings and errors

items.title: Implement Rate Limiting
items.description: Limit connection rate per IP to prevent brute force and DoS attacks.
items.threat: DoS, Brute Force - Unlimited requests exhaust resources.
items.impact: Service unavailability, successful brute force attacks.

implementation.apache:
# Load required modules:
a2enmod ratelimit

# In virtual host or .htaccess:
<Location />
    SetOutputFilter RATE_LIMIT
    SetEnv rate-limit 400
</Location>

# Or use mod_evasive:
DOSHashTableSize 3097
DOSPageCount 2
DOSSiteCount 50
DOSPageInterval 1
DOSSiteInterval 1
DOSBlockingPeriod 10

mitigationSteps: 1. Install and enable mod_evasive or mod_ratelimit
testing: Excessive requests receive 429 Too Many Requests
items.rollback: Disable rate limiting module or remove configuration.

items.title: Disable Unnecessary Modules and CGI
items.description: Disable modules and CGI execution that are not required.
items.threat: Expanded Attack Surface - Every enabled module is a potential vulnerability.

implementation.apache:
# List enabled modules:
apache2ctl -M

# Disable unnecessary modules:
a2dismod cgi cgid autoindex status

# Common modules to disable if unused:
a2dismod dav dav_fs userdir negotiation include

mitigationSteps: 5. Disable status module if public access not needed
testing: Only required modules listed in apache2ctl -M
